item.title: Iran Seeks Limited Interim Deal With US to Ease Economic Pressure
item.description: Iran is pushing for a limited interim agreement with the United States to ease mounting economic strain while avoiding major nuclear concessions, according to sources and analysts.

item.title: Iran Blames US Contradictions and Israeli Strikes for Diplomatic Delays
item.description: Iran says the slow pace of diplomacy to end the US-Iran conflict is due to a lack of trust, contradictory US positions, and continued Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

item.title: Ukraine Steps Up Attacks on Russian Energy Infrastructure
item.description: Kyiv has intensified attacks on Russian energy facilities as peace talks have failed to produce progress, targeting refineries and oil infrastructure across Russia.

item.title: Russia Increases Pressure on Armenia Ahead of Election
item.description: Russia is ramping up pressure on Armenia ahead of a June 7 election as the former close ally pursues deeper ties with Brussels and Washington.

item.title: South Korea Exports Surge to Four-Decade High on AI Chip Boom
item.description: South Korea's exports grew at the strongest annual rate in over 40 years in May as a global AI investment boom drove chip sales to record levels.

item.title: Iran Suspends US Message Exchanges, Threatens Strait of Hormuz Closure
item.description: Tehran halted indirect negotiations with Washington over Israeli operations in Lebanon, with state media warning Iran may block the critical oil shipping chokepoint.

item.title: Trump Says Iran "Really Wants to Make a Deal" as Diplomacy Continues
item.description: President Trump expressed optimism about ongoing negotiations with Tehran, stating a deal would benefit Washington and its allies amid the three-month-old conflict.

item.title: US Crude Exports Hit Record 5.6 Million Barrels Per Day
item.description: American oil exports reached unprecedented levels in May as Middle East supply disruptions from the Iran war drove demand from Asian and European refiners.

item.title: Zelenskiy Chief of Staff Sees Realistic Chance for Peace Deal by Winter
item.description: Ukrainian presidential chief of staff Kyrylo Budanov expressed optimism about reaching a peace agreement with Russia by winter, as Ukrainian drone attacks continue to disrupt Russian logistics.

item.title: France Intercepts Second Russian Shadow Fleet Oil Tanker
item.description: The French navy boarded a sanctioned vessel linked to Russian oil trade in the Atlantic, with President Macron announcing the operation backed by UK support. Moscow condemned the action as international piracy.

item.title: Chinas SAIC to Build First EU Car Plant in Spains Galicia
item.description: Chinese automaker SAIC Motor plans to establish its first production facility in the European Union in northwestern Spain, with an initial investment of approximately €200 million. The plant in Galicia aims to create around 1,000 direct jobs and become operational by 2028.

item.title: Senegals Ousted PM Sonko Says Party Wont Join New Government
item.description: Ousmane Sonko, Senegals recently ousted prime minister, announced his Pastef party will not participate in the new government after meeting with President Faye. The political crisis follows months of tensions amid economic challenges and an IMF program freeze over debt misreporting.

item.title: Alleged Iran-Backed Militant Shouts Were in a War in NYC Court
item.description: An Iraqi national and alleged Kataib Hezbollah member pleaded not guilty to charges of plotting attacks on US interests in Europe and America. Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi declared in court that children are being killed by US rockets, referring to a February strike on an Iranian girls school.

item.title: US Navy Opens New Munitions Innovation Hub in Maryland
item.description: The Navy broke ground on a new energetics innovation hub near Indian Head, Maryland to accelerate development of next-generation munitions. The facility will provide lab space for testing new explosives and propellant technologies critical to replenishing depleted weapons stockpiles.

item.title: GAO Slams Pentagon for Cutting 78,000 Civilians Without Analysis
item.description: A congressional watchdog report found the Defense Department shed 78,000 civilian employees in 2025 through resignations and layoffs with no consistent impact analysis and no plan to assess lessons learned. Morale among DOD employees has tanked during the current administration.

item.title: Two explosions strike cargo vessel in Gulf off Iraq
item.description: Two explosions hit a cargo vessel about 40 nautical miles southeast of Iraqs Umm Qasr, with one confirmed as a drone attack. The incident raises concerns about maritime security in the Gulf amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: Kenya protests US Ebola quarantine facility at military base
item.description: Hundreds protested in central Kenya against a US plan to set up an Ebola quarantine facility at a military base, after the High Court ordered a temporary suspension. The facility has drawn local opposition over safety concerns.

item.title: Crimea faces gasoline shortages after Ukrainian drone attacks
item.description: Drivers in Russian-controlled Crimea are grappling with gasoline rationing after Ukrainian drone attacks disrupted road supplies across southeastern Ukraine, affecting fuel distribution in the occupied peninsula.

item.title: Hezbollah Agrees to US Ceasefire Proposal with Israel
item.description: Lebanon's US embassy confirms Hezbollah accepted a US proposal for reciprocal cessation of attacks. The agreement would halt Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs in exchange for Hezbollah stopping attacks against Israel, with the framework to expand to all Lebanese territories.

item.title: US-China hold maritime military safety talks in Hawaii
item.description: The first US-China military communication since the Xi-Trump summit discussed avoiding misunderstandings during encounters between forces. Both sides agreed on the importance of effective communication for front-line forces.

item.title: France bans Israeli officials from Eurosatory defence show
item.description: France barred Israeli government representatives from the Paris arms exhibition and restricted Israeli companies to displaying only air defence systems. Israel's defence ministry called it a 'disgraceful decision' that puts France 'on the wrong side of history'.

item.title: Turkey emerges as Iran war winner through 'strategic ambiguity'
item.description: Turkey positioned itself as a key regional power and neutral mediator during the Iran war, benefiting from economic bridging and active non-alignment. Experts note Turkey's strategic flexibility has made it 'one of the winners' from regional turmoil.

item.title: UN agency blocks Trump official appointment over US arrears
item.description: The International Labour Organization rescinded the appointment of Sheng Li as deputy director-general because the US owes over million in unpaid membership contributions. The post is traditionally held by an American.

item.title: Appeals court rules Pentagon illegally banned transgender troops
item.description: A federal appeals court panel ruled that the Trump administration's policy unconstitutionally expelled transgender service members. The decision allows blocking current troop bans while permitting restrictions on new recruits.

item.title: Indian PM Modi Hosts Myanmar Junta Leader Min Aung Hlaing in New Delhi
item.description: Myanmar opposition groups criticized India for hosting the military leader, but India says engagement is the best way forward. This is Min Aung Hlaing's first visit to India since being sworn in as president in April.
